name: Sara Jacobs
subject: An update from our campaign on COVID-19
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Sara Jacobs for Congress
body:
I wanted to let you know that, out of an abundance of caution and a deep sense of responsibility to do what is best for the community, our campaign is instituting special operations to help stem the s

| [](https://go.sarajacobsforca.com/page/m/164b7902/27c6da69/2b3e1149/12f150d/3861006926/VEsH/?g=pdOgVDjfd5leRkt0m_fvHA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bp-sjc_footer?refcode=BLU-jacobs-header-textlink&recurring=true&amount=25")
---
| Friend,

I wanted to let you know that, out of an abundance of caution and a deep sense of responsibility to do what is best for the community, our campaign is instituting special operations to help stem the spread of COVID-19. We will continue to monitor CDC guidelines and change course accordingly.

We have implemented a "no handshake" policy and will do our best to practice social distancing while campaigning. As such, the campaign will not hold any events that convene more than 15 people at a time.

Additionally, starting on Saturday, our offices will be closed to the public until further notice. All our staff are encouraged to maintain good hygiene practices - regularly wash their hands, avoid contact with sick individuals, and stay home if they are feeling unwell. Any staff member that is sick should stay home and remain home until fully recovered. Any days spent at home because of illness will be fully paid.

These precautions aren't just to protect ourselves. They protect members of our community who are most at risk and help make sure our health care system isn't overloaded.

Unfortunately, 1 in 4 workers in the U.S. don't have paid sick leave and are forced to choose between their health -- and the health of others -- and their livelihood. The number is even higher for low-wage workers: 7 in 10 low-wage workers do not receive paid sick leave.

It's time for our government to mandate paid sick leave. _**[Sign your name if you agree.](https://go.sarajacobsforca.com/page/m/164b7902/27c6da69/2b3e1149/12f150e/3861006926/VEsE/?g=pdOgVDjfd5leRkt0m_fvHA "https://sarajacobs.bsd.net/j/step/its-time-for-paid-sick-leave")**_

Paid sick leave is a moral issue, but it also is proven to slow the transmission of diseases. One study found that paid sick leave reduces the spread of the seasonal flu, which is transmitted in the same ways as COVID-19.

It shouldn't take a global epidemic for the government and employers to implement paid sick leave, but we have a chance now to make change to help us fight COVID-19, and help our communities stay healthier in the future. _**[Join me in calling for paid sick leave today.](https://go.sarajacobsforca.com/page/m/164b7902/27c6da69/2b3e1149/12f150e/3861006926/VEsF/?g=pdOgVDjfd5leRkt0m_fvHA "https://sarajacobs.bsd.net/j/step/its-time-for-paid-sick-leave")**_
---
| [SIGN YOUR NAME ](https://go.sarajacobsforca.com/page/m/164b7902/27c6da69/2b3e1149/12f150e/3861006926/VEsC/?g=pdOgVDjfd5leRkt0m_fvHA "https://sarajacobs.bsd.net/j/step/its-time-for-paid-sick-leave")
---
Thank you for taking action,

Sara

* * *
| | [](https://go.sarajacobsforca.com/page/m/164b7902/27c6da69/2b3e1149/12f150f/3861006926/VEsD/?g=pdOgVDjfd5leRkt0m_fvHA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bp-sjc_footer?refcode=BLU-jacobs-footer-button&recurring=true&amount=25")

---
Paid for by Sara Jacobs for Congress

Sara Jacobs is running for Congress in California's 53rd Congressional District. In Congress, she will fight for policies to address climate change, guarantee health care as a human right, end the epidemic of gun violence plaguing our schools and communities, and defend access to reproductive care. Sara is rejecting all corporate PAC money in this campaign. She relies on grassroots supporters like you! _[You can make a contribution to her campaign here.](https://go.sarajacobsforca.com/page/m/164b7902/27c6da69/2b3e1149/12f1570/3861006926/VEsA/?g=pdOgVDjfd5leRkt0m_fvHA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bp-sjc_footer?refcode=BLU-jacobs-footer-textlink&recurring=true&amount=25")_

| [DONATE TO SARA ](https://go.sarajacobsforca.com/page/m/164b7902/27c6da69/2b3e1149/12f150f/3861006926/VEsO/?g=pdOgVDjfd5leRkt0m_fvHA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bp-sjc_footer?refcode=BLU-jacobs-footer-button&recurring=true&amount=25")

name: Rick Kennedy
subject: My Statement on the Coronavirus Pandemic
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rick Kennedy for Congress
body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16033506/169889699/-1907457918?refcode=03042020_em_hdr&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83NDEzOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNzQyZmU1MC0yNDY1LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=81rF-WyedT5CP7FzHDKEjEzwG3ljuEYRwSc63WnPogo=)
---
Dear Friend, Please find below our press release from yesterday. To protect the health and safety of our team and the general public, we'll be running an entirely virtual campaign until further notice.
* * *
AUSTIN, TX, March 12, 2020 - Rick Kennedy's statement on campaigning during a pandemic: We are in unprecedented times with events often moving faster than we can absorb. But certain truths still hold--we must not panic, we must take all common sense precautions, we must listen to the facts, however scary, from our health care professionals, and we must look after each other. With these precepts in mind, we intend to campaign for the runoff election. Now, more than ever, we need experienced, calm & intelligent leadership in Congress. We are considering many options on how best to campaign. They include:
* Holding remote town halls
* Offering one-on-one FaceTime calls with Rick during selected times
* Online forums with county party members
* Ramping up phone banking
* Current event messaging for our texting teams
* Pausing all blockwalking activities until the situation improves and best practices can be implemented

In closing, we don't know how long these times will last, so let's be kind to one another.
* * *
I speak often about how most policy decisions seem to be aimed at winning the next election rather than making sure we are as strong and prosperous in 50 years as we are now. And about how recent policy decisions have left us less prepared to confront a crisis as such as the one we now face. I only wish we been handed a less stark scenario to demonstrate the need for calm, logical, fact-based and forward-looking leadership in Washington. **[Please help us get there.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16033507/169889700/-1907457918?refcode=03132020_em_line&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83NDEzOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNzQyZmU1MC0yNDY1LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=81rF-WyedT5CP7FzHDKEjEzwG3ljuEYRwSc63WnPogo=)** | [$10](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16033508/169889701/1282640063?refcode=03132020_em_button&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83NDEzOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNzQyZmU1MC0yNDY1LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=81rF-WyedT5CP7FzHDKEjEzwG3ljuEYRwSc63WnPogo=) | [$25](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16033509/169889702/1282640063?refcode=03132020_em_button&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83NDEzOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNzQyZmU1MC0yNDY1LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=81rF-WyedT5CP7FzHDKEjEzwG3ljuEYRwSc63WnPogo=) | [$50](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16033510/169889703/1282640063?refcode=03132020_em_button&amount=50&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83NDEzOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNzQyZmU1MC0yNDY1LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=81rF-WyedT5CP7FzHDKEjEzwG3ljuEYRwSc63WnPogo=)

name: Mike Siegel
subject: The right to vote is our most fundamental right in a democratic society
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MIKE SIEGEL FOR CONGRESS](https://click
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16037249/169925472/683764388?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3M2RjOWMwOC0zMzY1LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KceeM8gO_-TTSQIroFT2BZIfUqu6UvSp8Era1T_YtU4=&emci=72dc9c08-3365-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=73dc9c08-3365-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=6233172)
---
Peter,

**We are in a crisis. **

The coronavirus is exposing some of the cracks in our social safety net. The federal government and the Texas state government have been far too slow to react, leaving cities and private companies to take necessary steps to slow the spread of COVID-19. There are large implications for federal inaction.

**One of those implications is our upcoming elections.** Poll workers and voters may be scared to go to the polls. Our most vulnerable citizens will be at the greatest risk: older Americans, people who are immunocompromised, and people who simply cannot afford to get sick and miss work. Their voices may not be heard at the ballot box — and that is unacceptable.

**Texas is one of the few states that does not allow “excuse-free” vote-by-mail, and we need to change that. Let’s expand our voting rights and push for universal vote-by-mail.**

The right to vote is our most fundamental right in a democratic society. We cannot let that right be threatened by this public health crisis. There is too much at stake, for our health, for our democracy, and for our future.

When I am in Congress, I will fight for legislation that would guarantee universal access to vote-by-mail. And until then, I am fighting here in Texas to push county and state officials to expand voting by mail during the May 26 runoff election and the November general election.

**[I need your help so I can take the fight for voting rights to Washington, DC. Donate right now so we can make sure that every vote counts.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16037250/169925473/203122784?refcode=em031320&recurring=1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3M2RjOWMwOC0zMzY1LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KceeM8gO_-TTSQIroFT2BZIfUqu6UvSp8Era1T_YtU4=) **

With love and respect,
Mike

clean_body: Peter, We are in a crisis. The coronavirus is exposing some of the cracks in our social safety net. The federal government and the Texas state government have been far too slow to react, leaving cities and private companies to take necessary steps to slow the spread of COVID-19. There are large implications for federal inaction. One of those implications is our upcoming elections. Poll workers and voters may be scared to go to the polls. Our most vulnerable citizens will be at the greatest risk: older Americans, people who are immunocompromised, and people who simply cannot afford to get sick and miss work. Their voices may not be heard at the ballot box — and that is unacceptable. Texas is one of the few states that does not allow “excuse-free” vote-by-mail, and we need to change that. Let’s expand our voting rights and push for universal vote-by-mail. The right to vote is our most fundamental right in a democratic society. We cannot let that right be threatened by this public health crisis. There is too much at stake, for our health, for our democracy, and for our future. When I am in Congress, I will fight for legislation that would guarantee universal access to vote-by-mail. And until then, I am fighting here in Texas to push county and state officials to expand voting by mail during the May 26 runoff election and the November general election. = With love and respect, Mike ** = PO Box 2409 Austin, TX 78701

name: Teresa Tomlinson
subject: COVID-19
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TERESA TOMLINSON FOR SENATE
body:
Yesterday, Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp spoke to the media after confirming Georgia’s first death to the COVID-19 pandemic, a 67-year old man with underlying health issues in Cobb County. | |
---

| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16048362/170023197/1077406611?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NDI2NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmNmIzZWQzMS01NTY1LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qq0pcHy2XnquIzJxFre2KWofxoDwtIyupOZqm0vZS9U=&emci=3e1d23f6-5465-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=f6b3ed31-5565-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=463701) **** | | Friend –

Yesterday, Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp spoke to the media after confirming Georgia’s first death to the **[COVID-19 pandemic](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16048363/170023198/1077406611?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NDI2NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmNmIzZWQzMS01NTY1LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qq0pcHy2XnquIzJxFre2KWofxoDwtIyupOZqm0vZS9U=&emci=3e1d23f6-5465-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=f6b3ed31-5565-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=463701)** , a 67-year old man with underlying health issues in Cobb County. He announced that Georgia public colleges will be closed for two weeks and is recommending schools and daycares close as early as today, as well as for seniors to avoid large crowds as a precaution.

I want to first extend my sincerest sympathies to the family of the gentleman who passed and urge others who have tested positive or may have been exposed to the coronavirus to take every precaution possible to ensure their health. Our campaign is taking preventative measures to mitigate the spread of the virus, including the suspension of our rallies and town halls that have brought me so much joy as I’ve traveled across our state. We will be converting many of these to “virtual” campaign stops, so stay tuned for more information on how you can participate in future online events.

As not only the former Mayor of Columbus, GA, but also as its former Public Safety Director and Head of Homeland Security, I am taking this public health crisis extremely seriously. What I offer as your next U.S. Senator is a steady hand with experience to help guide our government’s response during emergencies such as this.

For eight years as the chief elected officer of the Columbus Consolidated Government and the liaison with Ft. Benning, I worked with five commanding generals and have maintained a National Security Secret Clearance. While the response to COVID-19 is new to us all, I have run exercises formulating a government response to crises in my capacity as Mayor and Head of Homeland security. I believe this experience is invaluable.

Georgia deserves a Senator with the ability to lead, not learn on the fly or promote misinformation. As the home of the Centers for Disease Control (CDC) and a global airline hub in Atlanta, we have a unique responsibility to communicate to the nation, work with government stakeholders, and create a plan to isolate and contain this virus.

I would also be remiss not to express my frustration with an unjust system that, in this moment of crisis, bails out the most well-off among us, while millions of people struggle to navigate daily life and millions more lack basic healthcare coverage.

It was just announced that the Federal Reserve would be pumping $1.5 TRILLION into short-term funding for banking institutions. There was no question of how we would pay for it, yet somehow that question arises when it comes to paid sick leave, free childcare, universal healthcare, or even accessing testing kits that are critical to preventing the spread of this virus.

Our present situation is untenable and unacceptable. I could never have predicted this situation when I began this campaign, but it is one that has made me more determined than ever to lead us through it to build the safe, healthy, prosperous society we all deserve.

Yours truly,
Teresa Tomlinson ********************
---

* * *

PAID FOR BY TERESA TOMLINSON FOR SENATE
PMB 263, 3201 Macon Rd Ste 139, Columbus, GA 31906

This message was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com.
Contributions or gifts to Teresa Tomlinson for Senate are not deductible for federal income tax purposes.
We believe that emails are a crucial way for our campaign to stay in touch with supporters like you. However, if you'd prefer to receive fewer emails, you can [click here](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16048364/170023199/-1250957709?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NDI2NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmNmIzZWQzMS01NTY1LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qq0pcHy2XnquIzJxFre2KWofxoDwtIyupOZqm0vZS9U=&emci=3e1d23f6-5465-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=f6b3ed31-5565-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=463701). If you would like to unsubscribe, [click here](https://email.ngpvan.com/unsubscribeUnique/3e1d23f6-5465-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd/f6b3ed31-5565-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NDI2NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmNmIzZWQzMS01NTY1LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qq0pcHy2XnquIzJxFre2KWofxoDwtIyupOZqm0vZS9U=&id=100930058&emailAddress=dpwillis67%40gmail.com). Thanks for your support!

I would also be remiss not to express my frustration with an unjust system that, in this moment of crisis, bails out the most well-off among us, while millions of people struggle to navigate daily life and millions more lack basic healthcare coverage. It was just announced that the Federal Reserve would be pumping $1.5 TRILLION into short-term funding for banking institutions. There was no question of how we would pay for it, yet somehow that question arises when it comes to paid sick leave, free childcare, universal healthcare, or even accessing testing kits that are critical to preventing the spread of this virus. Our present situation is untenable and unacceptable. I could never have predicted this situation when I began this campaign, but it is one that has made me more determined than ever to lead us through it to build the safe, healthy, prosperous society we all deserve. Yours truly, Teresa Tomlinson **************** PAID FOR BY TERESA TOMLINSON FOR SENATE PMB 263, 3201 Macon Rd Ste 139, Columbus, GA 31906

body:

Hi Peter

Remember the Constitution?

Well, thus week, several "Blue states"- including Washington State- have banned Church services. This has only happened in Blue states!

The use of the state’s emergency powers will affect “social, recreational, spiritual” and other events including major sports events, concerts, festivals, conventions, religious services, and fundraisers. | A Message from Matt!
---
We are now more than a month into our campaign to bring a Conservative New Hampshire voice back to Congress and our team is growing quickly. In fact, we just released 104 new public endorsements, bringing our _total up to_ _208 local leaders and activists_ publicly supporting our campaign. Granite Staters are quickly realizing that I am the _True New Hampshire_ candidate, and that I don't need a map to find North Conway, Hampton Beach or Lake Winnipesaukee! Please take a look at my recent Op-Ed about ending Sanctuary Cities that ran in the Union Leader and the Granite Grok, stay tuned for more updates with the latest from our campaign and as always, thank you all for your support! \- Matt

name: Josh Hicks
subject: Our campaign is taking precautions to keep folks safe, but our work isn’t stopping.
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Josh Hicks for Congress
body:
secure.ngpvan.com/jOLn6GO-30ehw6Lc3tu2Kw2 [secure.ngpvan.com/jOLn6GO-30ehw6Lc3tu2Kw2]
In a time like this, we need real leadership. When we elect someone to go to Washington, that person’s job is to represent the people who live in their district.
It doesn’t matter if there’s an R or an D after your name on the ballot, that’s your job.
That’s always been part of why I’m running for Congress, and it’s more important right now than ever before.
Our campaign has been built on getting out in the district, talking to voters, and hearing from you.
That work is resonating with voters and donors, and we have a real pathway to winning this race.
And with a public health crisis looming, what I’m talking about with folks on the campaign trail — health care, good jobs and higher wages, and corporate influence — is more important than ever.
Our campaign is taking precautions to keep everyone safe, and we’ve postponed our upcoming events — but our work isn’t stopping.
We still need to keep up two big pieces of our work: Talking to and hearing from folks all over this district and raising money ahead of our first FEC deadline of the year.
First: We’re looking into virtual ways to connect with folks in KY-06 in the coming days to hear from you and work on building solutions together. If you want to get involved in that work, or find out how you can support this campaign from home, let me know that you’re in now by signing up here. [https://secure.ngpvan.com/jOLn6GO-30ehw6Lc3tu2Kw2]
I'm in to flip KY-06 [https://secure.ngpvan.com/jOLn6GO-30ehw6Lc3tu2Kw2]
Second: I understand if you’re unable to donate to a campaign right now. I’m grateful to have your support, financial or otherwise.
If you can, we still need you to chip in to our campaign before the end of the quarter. We will file our fundraising report with the FEC after March 31st — and we’re still on track for our best fundraising quarter to date.
I can chip in $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hicks-march?refcode=20200313_em_non_cv&amount=10]In the coming days, you’ll hear from my team and me about the exact adjustments we’re making on our team to change up our campaign.
But I’ll make this promise to you know: We’re not giving up and we won’t back down from standing up for this district and fighting for you. More now than ever, we need representatives who will fight for those too often forgotten by Washington politicians.
-- Josh
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jhicks-march?refcode=20200313_em_non_cv [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jhicks-march?refcode=20200313_em_non_cv]
Paid For By Josh Hicks for Congress
Josh Hicks for Congress
PO Box 1214
Lexington, KY 40588
United States
Josh Hicks is a Democrat running to represent Kentucky’s 6th Congressional District. He will be a representative who puts the needs of his constituents front and center in Washington. Josh grew up in Fleming County before leaving to serve in the Marines. After two deployments, he returned to Kentucky to serve his community as a police officer and attorney.
He’s running to represent hardworking, everyday Kentuckians. Not corporations. That means his campaign is relying on grassroots donations — lots of small donations from emails like this — not big checks from corporate special interests. Please consider making a $5 contribution to Josh’s campaign today. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jhicks-march?refcode=20200313_em_non_cv]
DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jhicks-march?refcode=20200313_em_non_cv]

name: Mary Mancini
subject: TNDP Weekly Recap: Wash Your Hands
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE TENNESSEE DEMOCRATIC PARTY, DR
body:
Chair, Tennessee Democratic Party
REPRESENTATIVE HODGES
PASSES INSULIN CAP BILL
With bipartisan support, Democratic Representative Jason Hodges successfully passed an insulin price cap bill. Limiting the out of pocket cost for insulin to $100.00 a month will protect those who suffer from diabetes from price gouging by drug makers. Unsurprisingly, the only "no" vote was from Republican Tim Rudd, who is a diabetic himself. [https://twitter.com/TheTNHoller/status/1238105624195579905?s=20]
https://twitter.com/TheTNHoller/status/1238105624195579905?s=20 [https://twitter.com/TheTNHoller/status/1238105624195579905?s=20]
JUST A REMINDER: WE MADE HISTORY IN
THE PRESIDENTIAL PRIMARY
We applaud each and every one of the 515,425 Tennessee democrats who cast their vote in the 2020 presidential primary - an increase of 38% over 2016.
We also want to thank the hard working volunteers, campaign staff, candidates, and county parties for all the hours and work put into getting people fired up and out to the polls.
OVER 1500 ATTENDED DEMOCRATIC
PARTY COUNTY CONVENTIONS
This past Saturday, Democrats across Tennessee took part in county conventions as part of the process to elect delegates to this summer’s DNC convention in Milwaukee. Democrats elected the selectors who will go on to congressional district conventions and vote on DNC delegates. We are excited to announce that more than 1,500 Democrats participated on Saturday.
Tennesseeans are tired of Trump’s rule and the harm his administration has caused Tennessee families. We saw it on Super Tuesday and now we saw it this weekend during the County Convention. People are turning out in historic numbers to participate in Democracy and vote Trump out.
Visit the Delegate Apportionment Calculations [https://s3.amazonaws.com/collective-agency-wp-uploads/wp-content/uploads/sites/5/2020/03/2020-Tennessee-Democratic-Delegate-Apportionment-Calculations.pdf] ! [https://s3.amazonaws.com/collective-agency-wp-uploads/wp-content/uploads/sites/5/2020/03/2020-Tennessee-Democratic-Delegate-Apportionment-Calculations.pdf]
TNDP TO HOLD CONGRESSIONAL
CONVENTIONS REMOTELY
In response to the CoronaVirus Pandemic, out of an abundance of caution, and to reduce social density, promote social distancing, and in light of meeting locations canceling scheduled events, the Tennessee Democratic Party will be switching from in-person Congressional District Conventions to remote conventions using a teleconference system.
Remote congressional district convention caucuses will still be held on Saturday, March 21st. Start times will be staggered throughout the day with the first caucuses estimated to start at 9 am central. Participants have been notified of the change and the TNDP will continue updating the public through social media. Please contact Party Affairs Director Maria Brewer at maria@tndp.org with questions.
“The Tennessee Democratic Party is prioritizing the health of all Tenneasseans during this time. It is during times like these our commitment to democracy and all people is so vital. We stand with Tennesseans every step of the way as this situation develops.” - Mary Mancini, TNDP Chair
IF YOU DON'T KNOW, NOW YOU KNOW:
PERMIT-LESS GUN CARRY
https://tndp.org/podcast/ [https://tndp.org/podcast/]
Chair Governor Bill Lee and Republicans in the legislature are putting Tennessee lives at risk by pushing their permit-less gun carry legislation. With mass shootings at an all time high and Tennessee gun deaths rising every single day, it is a time to strengthen gun control not become less strict. In this episode, Now You Know host and TNDP Chair, Mary Mancini, sits down with Safe Tennessee Project Executive Director, Beth Joslin Roth. Mary and Beth walk through 2nd Amendment Responsibility in Tennessee, the NRA's role in lawmaking, and Governor Lee's gun agenda. Listen now! [https://tndp.org/podcast/]
TWEETS OF THE WEEK
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP07/1/77134/images/Screen%20Shot%202020-03-13%20at%2011.22.37%20AM.png]
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP07/1/77134/images/Screen%20Shot%202020-03-13%20at%201.03.02%20PM.jpeg]
YOUR CONTRIBUTIONS IN ACTION
In the wake of the Middle Tennessee Tornado Tragedy, 21 polls across the state were closed. In Davidson County, many polling locations opened late. Thousands of people were unable to cast their ballot as planned, and yet the Secretary of State and Davidson County Election Commission would not expand poll times. This was not okay and would cause irreparable damage to democracy. TNDP filed a lawsuit for injunction to have poll times extended to make up for these delays. We won the lawsuit and kept polls open as late as 10PM. This is the exact work your contributions fund. Your contributions protect Tennessee's right to cast their ballots for Democrats and provide the staff and resources needed to do the work. Will you consider donating today? [https://secure.ngpvan.com/oNKhpMgsTk-zA4pXE2fn4w2]

name: Margaret Good
subject: Update on COVID-19
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY MARGARET GOOD FOR CONGRESS
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16052020/170061653/-940294256?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83ODIwMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhNWJiZmQyYy02MzY1LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=GvXazj8IVE2RI9WWbtvDEqs3CKLdRlgupnB9S4QHHfM=&emci=acdd1113-6065-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=a5bbfd2c-6365-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=9814644) Dear team, I am writing to you today to discuss the Coronavirus. Sarasota and Manatee have now both had patients test positive for COVID-19. I know there are a lot of concerns, many of which come from lack of information. I, too, am concerned and wanted to take a moment to address some of the issues I see and the actions we can take to keep our communities safe. Neither the federal government, nor our state government, are sufficiently addressing this public health crisis. While budget negotiations are underway at the state level, no plan has been formally communicated on what funding for Coronavirus will look like. Prior to the outbreak, the initial House budget proposal cut about $17 million dollars from the Disease Control and Prevention Program--the program that is responsible for this type of response. This is after decades of cuts to our public health funding. Between 2010 and 2018, Florida has cut 3,500 county health jobs. I am advocating for increased state funding to ensure that our residents not only receive testing for the virus, but also the healthcare they need to combat it. I am calling on our federal and state governments to act: The federal government should allow the use of a quick turnaround Coronavirus blood test like those being used in Japan, Italy, and China. In the meantime, Florida should be transparent about the number of tests we have available and provide projections of how many test kits we expect to receive in the coming days. Once we understand that, we should expand our testing criteria so that as many potentially infected residents can get tested as possible. Because we don’t have information about the scope of the virus, we don’t know who may be carrying the virus--and that’s why it’s important to limit social contact. From a public health perspective, the immediate objective should be to aggressively limit the spread of Coronavirus. This is important because the more we can slow down the spread, the more we increase the likelihood our healthcare system is not overwhelmed and is able to adequately treat patients. My message is this: Stay home as much as possible. Stay calm, but take this seriously. We have a real opportunity and a small window to slow the spread of this virus. We should take it. If you have any symptoms of COVID-19, please call the 24/7 Florida Department of Health hotline at **1 (866) 779-6121** , or email **COVID-19@flhealth.gov**. If you have problems getting help, please call our office at 941-955-8077. The Florida Department of Health published a toolkit that provides helpful information: [http://www.floridahealth.gov/diseases-and-conditions/COVID-19/covid19-toolkit.html](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16052021/170061654/763291455?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83ODIwMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhNWJiZmQyYy02MzY1LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=GvXazj8IVE2RI9WWbtvDEqs3CKLdRlgupnB9S4QHHfM=&emci=acdd1113-6065-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=a5bbfd2c-6365-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=9814644) Sincerely, Margaret
---

name: Kathy Manning
subject: COVID-19 and NC
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kathy Manning for Congress
body:
What you need to know:
|
---

| | I want to take a moment to share some information with each of you during this time of concern. As of now, fifteen people have tested presumptive positive for coronavirus in North Carolina. This includes two people in Forsyth County. There will likely be more confirmed cases in the days and weeks to come. Governor Cooper has just recommended that all gatherings of 100 or more people be postponed, and that employers allow people to work from home where possible. Our job as North Carolinians is to help slow the spread of coronavirus by taking a few critical steps: 1\. Wash your hands and frequently touched surfaces.
2\. Stay home if you are sick.
3\. Practice social distancing and avoid large gatherings. By helping to slow the spread of the virus, we're helping ensure that our hospitals and medical professionals don't become overwhelmed with cases that could have been prevented. These measures are critical to our national health but they will also cause economic uncertainty for many low and middle income families in the Sixth District and across the country. In order to help curb the economic impact on families, Congress must pass legislation to help those individuals and businesses impacted the most severely by these necessary measures, including emergency sick leave, increased unemployment benefits and food assistance, free Coronavirus testing for those who need testing, and protections against price gouging. The federal government must make sure coronavirus tests are available to states in need, including North Carolina, and that test results are provided quickly. We are all in this together, so take all necessary precautions and take a moment to check on a neighbor or a friend who may need assistance. My team is currently tele-working but we will continue to provide updated information. Together, we will get through this. For more information please visit NC DHHS' website at _**[http://bit.ly/2TJjPP6](https://secure.kathymanningfornc.com/page/m/18d9d341/3d199a51/2f6d6b23/7364f6c2/266068803/VEsH/?g=Bm8FRTjfd5ld5gouxTH21cA "http://bit.ly/2TJjPP6")**_ or the CDC website at [_**http://bit.ly/2QcsY0F**_](https://secure.kathymanningfornc.com/page/m/18d9d341/3d199a51/2f6d6b23/7364f6c3/266068803/VEsE/?g=Bm8FRTjfd5ld5gouxTH21cA "http://bit.ly/2QcsY0F"). Thank you, Kathy |
---
| | Paid for by Kathy Manning for Congress
---

| | Paid for by Kathy Manning for Congress
---

name: Merideth VanSant
subject: What Coronavirus has taught us
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Merideth VanSant for Congress
body:
---
| | | | | | [ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0019m4jby3lotkfe3BYRgyPUYLYRea9RB4J8fHLXa4w9Pi_tvspKV3Oek-6QXPT4K80agMHgr56fcw5eRmfUjDI4xcC3UrRa8XzQUfSiBFw1k2bRpETtQ9KHmo5NcJObZ5pLnOrUP0Fp50xif0owqcrQZeghoKy8VoX&c=UY8g6A4oSCb9vqmLKGMKxJ61ktebHmiSY7uPAa_LpIFB_ix9c5_JwQ==&ch=q2X9waGjjffLwUZBa-wBupl-y28Gg_bW7WaScVVi9jUGdCY2Nj0SaA==)
---
| |
---
| Hello!
Every situation is an opportunity to learn and the COVID-19 outbreak is no different.
It has emphasized how fear has far more sway in our markets than facts. It's shown us how truly global our market is.

Events like this in our capital markets are inevitable, and solid, steady handed leadership is imperative at the local, state, and federal level. It's one of the top reasons I'm running for Congress.
Below is a video from the Oklahoma State Department of Health on basic coronavirus facts and recommended prevention measures. I encourage you to watch it and share to help make sure accurate, up-to-date information is what people hear.
---

name: =?utf-8?Q?SOCIAL_SECURITY_ALERT_=28via_Ben_Ray_Luj=C3=A1n=29?=
subject: Donald Trump just announced:
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by People for Ben
body:
Do you believe our seniors' health care should be SLASHED?
---
| | [**TRUMP BUDGET SLASHES MEDICARE AND SOCIAL SECURITY**](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b659/18e32a7f/4ea337f7/7b2239b5/3287594438/VEsH/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/s/medicare-survey?source=20200313_em_pet_b1_ssavbrl_ia") | I'm absolutely disgusted. President Trump's budget completely SLASHES Social Security and Medicare -- [_**when seniors are relying on these life-saving benefits more than ever.**_](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b659/18e32a7f/4ea337f7/7b2239b5/3287594438/VEsE/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/s/medicare-survey?source=20200313_em_pet_b1_ssavbrl_ia") [**I need 10,000 responses before midnight: Do you believe our seniors ' health care should be SLASHED?**](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b659/18e32a7f/4ea337f7/7b2239b5/3287594438/VEsF/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/s/medicare-survey?source=20200313_em_pet_b1_ssavbrl_ia") [**NO WAY!! >>**](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b659/18e32a7f/4ea337f7/7b2239b5/3287594438/VEsC/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/s/medicare-survey?source=20200313_em_pet_b1_ssavbrl_ia")** **[Yes](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b659/18e32a7f/4ea337f7/7b2239b5/3287594438/VEsD/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/s/medicare-survey?source=20200313_em_pet_b1_ssavbrl_ia")****
---

I'm furious.

President Trump just released a despicable new budget that SLASHES Medicare and Social Security.

The worst part? [**Our seniors have paid into Medicare their entire lives. Now, they 're relying on it to survive.**](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b659/18e32a7f/4ea337f7/7b2239b5/3287594438/VEsA/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/s/medicare-survey?source=20200313_em_pet_b1_ssavbrl_ia")

We CANNOT let them pay the price for Republicans' reckless actions.

[_**BEFORE MIDNIGHT: I need 10,000 immediate responses on my Medicare Poll to OPPOSE these heartless cuts. Will you record your pro-Medicare response right away? Our seniors need us to have their backs. >>**_](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b659/18e32a7f/4ea337f7/7b2239b5/3287594438/VEsB/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/s/medicare-survey?source=20200313_em_pet_b1_ssavbrl_ia")

Thank you,

Ben Ray

---
[](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b659/18e32a7f/4ea337f7/7b2239aa/3287594438/VEsO/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://www.facebook.com/BenRayLujan") [](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b659/18e32a7f/4ea337f7/7b2239ab/3287594438/VEsP/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://twitter.com/benraylujan") [](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b659/18e32a7f/4ea337f7/7b2239a8/3287594438/VEsHBQ/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://www.instagram.com/benraylujan/?hl=en")
Ben Ray Lujan is running to be the first Hispanic Senator from New Mexico in more than 40 years. No one like Ben Ray has ever run for Senate before. He led the charge to take back the House for the American people and Democrats (and succeeded by a historic margin) and now he's running to take back the Senate too.

We know we send you a lot of emails, but that's because Ben Ray isn't taking a cent of corporate PAC money. His campaign is 100% people-powered. And the polls show we have a once-in-a-lifetime shot to finally defeat Mitch McConnell and take back the Senate. But we can't do this without winning New Mexico. Will you be a part of this historic movement? **[DONATE](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b659/18e32a7f/4ea337f7/7b2239a9/3287594438/VEsHBA/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/brl_email?refcode=email_footer_donate&amount=25&recurring=on&recur_weekly=on")**
We're counting on you to stick with us for the long-haul. If you want to stay updated but receive fewer emails, [_**please let us know.**_](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b659/18e32a7f/4ea337f7/7b2239ae/3287594438/VEsHBw/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/s/fewer-emails")

If you'd like to unsubscribe completely, we understand.
Please unsubscribe [_here_](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b659/18e32a7f/4ea337f7/7b2239af/3287594438/VEsHBg/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://secure.benrlujan.com/unsubscribe"), and we'll make sure to process your request.

People for Ben
PO Box 25371
Albuquerque, NM 87125

name: Team Sri
subject: Re: The Time To Speak Up Is Now!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Sri for Congress
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/mBUcOz2tZHTVE5lU6iV5kLlkIUO90P0AkbMFeZzt6Fg=/pCnYOomIzIhYq5O_cJqjjHTjT9hi9REQ5ZkRhfDCIHc=?refcode=20200313&amount=25)
---
Team, Just wanted to check in to make sure that you had a chance to read Sri's message. It's never to late to stand up for what's right, and that means standing up for immigrants and ensuring that we're calling out anti-immigrant and xenophobic rhetoric when we see it. **Take a moment and add your name to Sri 's to call on his GOP opponents to stop their anti-immigrant rhetoric: [Sri2020.com/immigration](https://act.myngp.com/el/mBUcOz2tZHTVE5lU6iV5kLlkIUO90P0AkbMFeZzt6Fg=/E8bwUSAA_aOxyVs5aGvyaOHenUJUgpDdu_Ykq0UrQgY=?refcode=20200313).** Team Sri \---------Original Message--------- Team, While the other side is focusing on amplifying President Trump's message of xenophobia, hostility, and racism towards immigrant communities, our team is working to amplify the voices of the people that make our district, our neighborhoods, and our homes the incredible places they are and should be. Why is it acceptable to flaunt an anti-immigration message and call it a policy stance as opposed to what it really is: racist and xenophobic. Why is the other side unabashedly campaigning on continuing Trump's stance on separating children from their families? The time to speak up is now. [**Add your name to mine and call on my GOP opposition to end their anti-immigrant rhetoric. **](https://act.myngp.com/el/mBUcOz2tZHTVE5lU6iV5kLlkIUO90P0AkbMFeZzt6Fg=/E8bwUSAA_aOxyVs5aGvyaOHenUJUgpDdu_Ykq0UrQgY=?refcode=20200313) | ****** ** | ******[SIGN ON ⇨](https://act.myngp.com/el/mBUcOz2tZHTVE5lU6iV5kLlkIUO90P0AkbMFeZzt6Fg=/E8bwUSAA_aOxyVs5aGvyaOHenUJUgpDdu_Ykq0UrQgY=?refcode=20200313)******
---
********

One of my opponents fashions herself as "The Wall who will help Donald Trump build a wall!" But I know that Texans put one thing before everything else: our family. No matter our faith, our gender, the language we speak, who we love, or where we were born, we are united as Texans in our shared belief that our families come first.
And that means standing up for the many families that have been attacked relentlessly and can't face the hatred and bigotry alone.
It's time to stand up for immigrants' rights. Stand with me and add your name if you agree: **[Sri2020.com/immigration](https://act.myngp.com/el/mBUcOz2tZHTVE5lU6iV5kLlkIUO90P0AkbMFeZzt6Fg=/nBj20UDSBNk0t8Qfq7o_tuaaTqT8khVnLWNPQ5B_7-w=?refcode=20200313)**.
Thank you,

Sri Preston Kulkarni

**** ****
**STAY CONNECTED WITH OUR MOVEMENT** **[](https://act.myngp.com/el/mBUcOz2tZHTVE5lU6iV5kLlkIUO90P0AkbMFeZzt6Fg=/tuvEMwqxK3ErpDsg287nhjbXfi-tY1g-j-OwPBL5V_o=) [](https://act.myngp.com/el/mBUcOz2tZHTVE5lU6iV5kLlkIUO90P0AkbMFeZzt6Fg=/AYF4j_G0JZvzIPt9ibbaWQhCg-I_GTAwlOGdpoQn8-o=) [](https://act.myngp.com/el/mBUcOz2tZHTVE5lU6iV5kLlkIUO90P0AkbMFeZzt6Fg=/LeTIB-JAIFvAbNmjQjh2_EFgYib24g60Wfx43B78wrs=)** | Paid for by Sri for Congress.

name: Dick Durbin
subject: I want to share an update with you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Dick Durbin
body:
#
Peter,
I've made a decision which I want to share with my closest friends and supporters.
In light of the public health emergency we now face I am currently suspending my in-person fundraising events until this situation is stabilized.
I cannot in good conscience expose my friends to gatherings which might create any threat to their health.
I hope you understand that this is a decision which will impact our planned fundraising efforts.
And I hope I can count on you to help.
We are going to turn to emails and communications like this to sustain our efforts to raise the funds we need to wage a successful re-election campaign.
**[Here's my ask: can you pitch in $5 to help me continue my journey to victory in November?](https://act.durbinforsenate.%3D%3D/?g=ofPncVH8x8VcsCmaF7wmZzA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dd-mar-2020?refcode=em200313i&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&addr1=&addr2=&city=Englewood&state=FL&phone=&employer=&occupation=")**
Your contribution will help us build a lean, but effective campaign to deliver our message to the voters in Illinois.
So, first, stay well. And stay engaged. And help me win this campaign to get the job of change in Washington done the right way.
With gratitude for your loyal support,
Dick Durbin

**[CONTRIBUTE](https://act.durbinforsenate.%3D%3D/?g=ofPncVH8x8VcsCmaF7wmZzA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dd-mar-2020?refcode=em200313i&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&addr1=&addr2=&city=Englewood&state=FL&phone=&employer=&occupation=")**

* * *
Paid for by Friends of Dick Durbin
---

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Should you want to unsubscribe, you can [click here](https://act.durbinforsenate.com/page/m/45ff4180/42ab3cf4/60ed526b/135d5a3d/3085712352/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/?g=ofPncVH8x8VcsCmaF7wmZzA "http://action.dickdurbin.com/page/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com"). Join Senator Durbin in taking on powerful special interests on behalf of hard-working middle class families by [making a contribution to our campaign here.](https://act.durbinforsenate.com/page/m/45ff4180/42ab3cf4/60ed526b/135d5a3e/3085712352/VEsC/?g=ofPncVH8x8VcsCmaF7wmZzA "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/durbin_footer")
Friends of Dick Durbin
Attn: Online Department
101 W. Grand, Suite 200
Chicago, IL 60654

name: Eugene DePasquale
subject: COVID-19 Update
disclaimer_text: Paid for by DePasquale for PA 10
body:
We’re in a difficult time for our country, and I have been closely monitoring the situation here in Pennsylvania. I wanted to provide you some important information about how we can keep ourselves and our loved ones safe from the spread of the novel coronavirus known as COVID-19 and also provide some updates about our campaign.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16055404/170088749/1960319551?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NTE3NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNTlhYmU0Ny02ZTY1LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=xT9AxIcb34sBItNTAkb233ZdGS_g8skN372stwjX1Q8=&emci=201ce2df-6665-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=d59abe47-6e65-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=10203249) Friend,

We’re in a difficult time for our country, and I have been closely monitoring the situation here in Pennsylvania. I wanted to provide you some important information about how we can keep ourselves and our loved ones safe from the spread of the novel coronavirus known as COVID-19 and also provide some updates about our campaign.

In order to keep our communities healthy and safe, it is important we follow the guidelines laid out by Governor Tom Wolf and Secretary of Health Dr. Rachel Levine.

[**You can find up-to-date information from the state Department of Health here.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16055405/170088750/-1652651436?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NTE3NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNTlhYmU0Ny02ZTY1LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=xT9AxIcb34sBItNTAkb233ZdGS_g8skN372stwjX1Q8=&emci=201ce2df-6665-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=d59abe47-6e65-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=10203249)

Here are some small ways you can help control the spread of COVID-19 in your community (Source: [health.pa.gov](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16055406/170088751/-1783213624?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NTE3NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNTlhYmU0Ny02ZTY1LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=xT9AxIcb34sBItNTAkb233ZdGS_g8skN372stwjX1Q8=&emci=201ce2df-6665-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=d59abe47-6e65-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=10203249)):
* Cover coughs or sneezes with your elbow. **Do not use your hands!**
* Wash hands often with soap and water for at least 20 seconds. Use an alcohol-based hand sanitizer if soap and water are not available.
* Clean surfaces frequently, including countertops, light switches, cell phones, remotes, and other frequently touched items.
* Contain: if you are sick, stay home until you are feeling better.

Due to growing concerns surrounding the spread of coronavirus, our campaign has made the decision to suspend all in-person events for the month of March and will continue to reassess following state Department of Health guidelines. This includes canvassing.

[**For now, please fill out this form to sign up for phone banking from home!**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16055407/170088752/-709993875?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NTE3NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNTlhYmU0Ny02ZTY1LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=xT9AxIcb34sBItNTAkb233ZdGS_g8skN372stwjX1Q8=&emci=201ce2df-6665-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=d59abe47-6e65-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=10203249)

In the coming days and weeks, we'll be rolling out training webinars, a texting program, and other ways to get involved remotely utilizing relational and digital organizing techniques.

Finally, it’s important to remember that Pennsylvania now allows no-excuse vote-by-mail. I’d like to encourage as many people as possible to take advantage of this to avoid crowds and long lines for our primary election on April 28.

[**For instructions and to apply to vote-by-mail online, visit the Pennsylvania Department of State's website.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16055408/170088753/-1813083118?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NTE3NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNTlhYmU0Ny02ZTY1LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=xT9AxIcb34sBItNTAkb233ZdGS_g8skN372stwjX1Q8=&emci=201ce2df-6665-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=d59abe47-6e65-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=10203249#/OnlineAbsenteeBegin)

Thank you for your time and attention, and please stay safe and healthy!

clean_body: We’re in a difficult time for our country, and I have been closely monitoring the situation here in Pennsylvania. I wanted to provide you some important information about how we can keep ourselves and our loved ones safe from the spread of the novel coronavirus known as COVID-19 and also provide some updates about our campaign. Friend, We’re in a difficult time for our country, and I have been closely monitoring the situation here in Pennsylvania. I wanted to provide you some important information about how we can keep ourselves and our loved ones safe from the spread of the novel coronavirus known as COVID-19 and also provide some updates about our campaign. In order to keep our communities healthy and safe, it is important we follow the guidelines laid out by Governor Tom Wolf and Secretary of Health Dr. Rachel Levine. Here are some small ways you can help control the spread of COVID-19 in your community (Source: ): Cover coughs or sneezes with your elbow. Do not use your hands! Wash hands often with soap and water for at least 20 seconds. Use an alcohol-based hand sanitizer if soap and water are not available. Clean surfaces frequently, including countertops, light switches, cell phones, remotes, and other frequently touched items. Contain: if you are sick, stay home until you are feeling better. Due to growing concerns surrounding the spread of coronavirus, our campaign has made the decision to suspend all in-person events for the month of March and will continue to reassess following state Department of Health guidelines. This includes canvassing. In the coming days and weeks, we'll be rolling out training webinars, a texting program, and other ways to get involved remotely utilizing relational and digital organizing techniques. Finally, it’s important to remember that Pennsylvania now allows no-excuse vote-by-mail. I’d like to encourage as many people as possible to take advantage of this to avoid crowds and long lines for our primary election on April 28. Thank you for your time and attention, and please stay safe and healthy! \--Eugene DePasquale Follow Eugene on social media for campaign updates: Eugene DePasquale is running to represent Pennsylvania's 10th Congressional District. To learn more, please visit . | Paid for by DePasquale for PA 10 DePasquale for PA 10 P.O. subject: This week
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MESSNER FOR SENATE
body:
Tap or click to read more >>
[ ](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/2cff816c566216bc/55cec137af40535cc3d6b838967d376c/33153) [ Donate Now! ](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/2cff816c566216bc/d3fff4a0575a58d0c3d6b838967d376c/33153) [ Contact Us ](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/2cff816c566216bc/c47be662a802b1b7c3d6b838967d376c/33153) Entrepreneur. Veteran. Conservative. | |
---
**Response to President's Address on Coronavirus: "We must work together as one."**
After viewing President Trump’s address to the nation Thursday evening regarding the coronavirus, Corky noted: “The President tonight showed a measured calm approach to the Coronavirus pandemic. From Day One, he has taken decisive steps, like banning travel from China. Tonight’s unified message and concrete steps will no doubt help slow this outbreak and help lessen the economic impact on small businesses and their employees.”
“It is imperative we put politics aside and work together as one country. We are a strong country but we are stronger when we work together as one.”
|
---
**Corky Messner Invests an Additional $2M in Senate Campaign**
Corky Messner confirmed this week he is investing an additional $2 million into the race this quarter.

“New Hampshire Republicans must nominate a strong, well-funded candidate for Senate,” says Corky. “This is a Presidential battleground state and a rare opportunity for a Senate pickup in 2020. It will not be enough for a Republican Senate candidate to count on quickly ramping up fundraising for the general election after the primary, considering Senator Jeanne Shaheen currently has $5,750,000 cash on hand and New Hampshire’s primary is September 8 – a mere eight weeks before the general election.”
He continued that the Republican nominee in the race must have general election resources now. With the additional $2 million, Corky will end the first quarter of the year with approximately 10 times the cash on-hand advantage versus his Republican rivals.

“If the Republican nominee is not able to be on television and in mailboxes within days of securing the nomination, Shaheen and the Democrats will have a window of opportunity to define the Republican nominee in highly unfavorable terms. I am the only Republican in a position to defeat Jeanne Shaheen.”
|
---
**In the News**
WMUR’s Primary Source first reported Corky’s campaign investment. Writes John DiStaso, “The additional $2 million will be used to continue to ramp up his campaign in the early stages, with an eye toward a general election match-up with the very well-funded Democratic incumbent Jeanne Shaheen.”
Politico also cited the news item in its [**Morning Score**](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/2cff816c566216bc/f0301b1769caf9d3c3d6b838967d376c/33153) on March 13th, in its round-up of the latest political news of note.
|
---
**Quick Pic**
Corky spoke to the Hollis Republican Committee recently, explaining his positions on the issues and taking questions from an engaged, supportive group. Thank you, Hollis!
|
---

name: Mark Warner
subject: Important Update
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner
body:
My number one concern is the health and safety of my constituents.
|
---
| | Derek --

Today, I filed my required ballot-qualifying petitions with 33,279 signatures for my U.S. Senate re-election campaign. I am extremely thankful to the dedicated staff and volunteers who worked hard to get the campaign to this place.

You may have seen that I planned a campaign announcement tour across the Commonwealth for next week. I want to let you know that we have made the decision to **postpone this tour in an abundance of caution for public health.**

I am proud to file for re-election today, but my number one concern is the health and safety of my constituents. I have decided to postpone my announcement tour across the Commonwealth to be where Virginians need me most, serving them in my capacity as their United States Senator.

**In the meantime, we need everyone to pitch in to slow and stop the spread of coronavirus -- it's important that everyone has access to the resources they need to keep themselves and their communities healthy and safe.** Each of us has a critical role to play in slowing and stopping the spread of COVID-19 (coronavirus), whether or not we're considered at high risk ourselves.

Here are some key precautions that health professionals recommend taking at this time:
* **Wash your hands** often with soap and warm water for at least 20 seconds. (That's two rounds of "Happy Birthday.") Avoid touching your face, clean high-traffic surfaces in your home and workspace often, and refrain from sharing beverages or personal items.
* **Avoid large public gatherings** , especially if you are elderly or living with a compromised immune system. Social distancing is critical not only to avoid getting sick yourself, but to ensure that you do not unknowingly pass the virus to others.
* **Remain at home and call your doctor if you are sick.** The Virginia state government is advising Virginia residents to **[contact their local health department](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d591/53d9897e/5333a0b8/1c8994ed/547434979/VEsH/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "http://www.vdh.virginia.gov/health-department-locator/")** if they are exhibiting any symptoms of coronavirus including fever, cough, or trouble breathing.

During times like this, it's critical that everyone stays informed. **You can visit the CDC 's website for the latest coronavirus news and all that you need to know: [https://www.cdc.gov/coronavirus/2019-ncov/index.html](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d591/53d9897e/5333a0b8/1c8994ec/547434979/VEsE/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://www.cdc.gov/coronavirus/2019-ncov/index.html").**

And for the latest local updates, please see the **[Virginia Department of Health's COVID-19 guideline page](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d591/53d9897e/5333a0b8/1c8994eb/547434979/VEsF/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "http://www.vdh.virginia.gov/surveillance-and-investigation/novel-coronavirus/").**

Thank you for your vigilance.

-- Mark Warner
---
|
---

[](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d591/53d9897e/5333a0b8/1c8994ea/547434979/VEsC/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://markwarnerva.com") Friends of Mark Warner

Williamsburg, VA 23185

| | Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner

name: Team Bloomberg
subject: Comparing tweets
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mike Bloomberg 2020
body:
What Mike and Trump shared this week

| Friend -- Both Donald Trump and Mike were active on Twitter this week -- but there was a big difference in what they shared.

On Monday, Mike posted that he's pledging $2 million to help register 500,000 African American voters in eight key states and push back on voter suppression. The next day, Mike jumped on Twitter to share an initiative that will help mayors and local governments connect with experts to manage responses to the coronavirus outbreak. Yesterday, Mike tweeted about another pledge of $500,000 to help register Latino voters and get them to the polls.

What did Donald Trump tweet this week? With Americans concerned for their health and livelihoods, the president complained about the media, attacked Democrats, and shared a manipulated video.

When the president finally got off Twitter to speak with the American people about coronavirus, his Oval Office address was riddled with inaccuracies, offered few reassuring solutions, and sent markets tumbling with a lack of confidence in American leadership.

It has never been more important that we come together, get our country back on track, and put a real leader in the White House. That's going to take action -- not just talk and tweets.

We're not staying on the sidelines. And neither should you. Can you make sure we have your most up-to-date information so that we can stay in touch and keep this work going?

[**https://mikebloomberg.com/2020/defeat-trump-info-p-20200311/**](http://i.mikebloomberg.com/u/click?_t=8613ca2a3316453b8f979e9fa5797d01&_m=4965e6d572014c10878f9d86b541c006&_e=kLIbuA3I7ZAoKB688eOh76zWg6Kj-7pNQvmOsqyGJJA21MeEi4zKAELbBcOdhZzL_5D4Oa7myKncNCQ8uaCUpPUyCz6Kl3FcifQI7IB5_AJCdtnaszppB8pGdhYLk4VdoimUfp7pJ_FlumL14-KnoDiXUK2HUJwW9QOs3LRdlbQ2li5ccMPnQ9Xp1sjjnbOkCdyArGErOYAgvXkW_CUErH-jg31PjTQZAH3ttTTlBdWHiBGDabNyyFSF4ogB9gtCT389msyrrkNY-KTl0hPmSq4NkiFDS9W1BglPOQ51FPFywl6-HZb8u1OVlxYivYXCLK9i6Ar4aunMeal0k0L7idwIrDokmyBcWWMHWBhCGSZMuzpNkHUTOmnrZq9i5qvRqVmevyT_ihQp1wnP9l_uRQ%3D%3D) \- Team Bloomberg

clean_body: What Mike and Trump shared this week | Friend -- Both Donald Trump and Mike were active on Twitter this week -- but there was a big difference in what they shared. On Monday, Mike posted that he's pledging $2 million to help register 500,000 African American voters in eight key states and push back on voter suppression. The next day, Mike jumped on Twitter to share an initiative that will help mayors and local governments connect with experts to manage responses to the coronavirus outbreak. Yesterday, Mike tweeted about another pledge of $500,000 to help register Latino voters and get them to the polls. What did Donald Trump tweet this week? With Americans concerned for their health and livelihoods, the president complained about the media, attacked Democrats, and shared a manipulated video. When the president finally got off Twitter to speak with the American people about coronavirus, his Oval Office address was riddled with inaccuracies, offered few reassuring solutions, and sent markets tumbling with a lack of confidence in American leadership. It has never been more important that we come together, get our country back on track, and put a real leader in the White House. That's going to take action -- not just talk and tweets. We're not staying on the sidelines. And neither should you. Can you make sure we have your most up-to-date information so that we can stay in touch and keep this work going? \- Team Bloomberg | | | ---|---|--- . Paid for by Mike Bloomberg 2020 Mike Bloomberg 2020 909 Third Ave. New York, NY 10022 United States All content © 2020. All rights reserved.

I've come to a decision I want to share with you. | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/8e88ca493833/an-update-for-you?e=140a9b81e7)
---
| |
---
| |
#
Recently my campaign team and I sat down to discuss how we move forward with regards to our upcoming events in light of the COVID-19 pandemic.

We have come to a decision that I feel I need to share with you and all of our other supporters. **Our campaign is suspending or canceling all of our upcoming in-person fundraising events until this situation is stable. **

This decision will have a sizable impact on our original fundraising goals, and I hope that I can count on you to help mitigate that impact.

I'll get right to it. [Can you chip in today to help us reach our goals and send us on our way to a successful election in November?](https://daniforillinois.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=16d8d19eb3a417b5b09061c0d&id=07c58d2107&e=140a9b81e7)

Your donation to our campaign will help us create the type of campaign that will flip this seat from red to blue.

I am so grateful to have your support. Stay safe as we deal with this crisis together.

Dani Brzozowski
Democrat for Congress- IL 16

name: West Virginia Democratic Party
subject: Statement on Coronavirus Precautions and Democratic County Conventions
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by the WV State Democratic Executive Committee (www
body:
**For Immediate Release:**
Friday, March 13, 2020
Contact: Brittni McGuire, [Brittni@wvdemocrats.com](mailto:Brittni@wvdemocrats.com)

**Statement on Coronavirus Precautions and Democratic County Conventions **

(Charleston, WV) -- Today the West Virginia Democratic Party has released a statement regarding safety precautions and plans due to the Coronavirus outbreak:

"The health and safety of voters is our top priority and out of an abundance of caution, we are working with the DNC Rules and Bylaws Committee to determine the best course of action regarding our County Conventions coming up on March 28.

Again, the safety of our voters remains our top priority and as soon as we get final confirmation of plans to move forward from the DNC you will be notified. We want to be sure that everyone is safe, but everyone who wants to be involved in this process still has the opportunity to be included.

Aside from County Convention precautions and possible schedule changes, the West Virginia Democratic Party is taking other preventative measures to ensure the health and safety of Party staff and volunteers:

* West Virginia Democratic Party Headquarters is closed to the public.

* The 2020 Democratic Women's Summit originally scheduled for April 18th is postponed.

* Check with your local County Committees and Chairs for cancellations and schedule changes of fundraisers and events

Again, we will be updating you in the near future once we have more information."

**LINK TO WEBSITE PAGE THAT INCLUDES INFO AS IT BECOMES AVAILABLE: **

[https://wvdemocrats.com/coronavirus-precautions-and-updates/](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16057470/170111099/1587929711?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzgyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxYWI3ZDE5Yy03NzY1LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=b9VAyXKqlD0U--klzodbMgswJLMtNGWmxtwfwsb5yUc=&emci=e5a3c8e0-7665-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=1ab7d19c-7765-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=5094937)

###

I can’t believe the things I’ve seen over the past month.

Now, I’m watching as the Left continues to pour more money right here in Illinois– making our great state their next target. **They know conservatives here are ready to fight back and flip our state RED, but they’re trying everything they can to prevent that.**

They’re coming to silence conservatives across the state, and if we let them, they’ll be free to push their socialist agenda upon the people of Illinois and the rest of the country.

[**Will you make a difference by donating anything you can now?**](https://jerryforcongress.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7cd9c63b39d00967cc49e3ffc&id=da8918632d&e=9b692867fa)

I want to protect your Second Amendment rights.

But I can’t do any of those things in the House if I don’t make it – [**and I need your support NOW.**](https://jerryforcongress.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7cd9c63b39d00967cc49e3ffc&id=151ac183e7&e=9b692867fa)

[**So if you’re with me... if you know the importance of taking back the House... if you’re willing to do whatever it takes to preserve conservatism in America, I need you to have my back. **](https://jerryforcongress.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7cd9c63b39d00967cc49e3ffc&id=3387aa87d7&e=9b692867fa)

clean_body: | | | | | | | Friends, I can’t believe the things I’ve seen over the past month. First, I watched a self-proclaimed SOCIALIST ascend in national polls. Let that sink in. Now, I’m watching as the Left continues to pour more money right here in Illinois– making our great state their next target. They know conservatives here are ready to fight back and flip our state RED, but they’re trying everything they can to prevent that. They’re coming to silence conservatives across the state, and if we let them, they’ll be free to push their socialist agenda upon the people of Illinois and the rest of the country. | "FLIP ILLINOIS RED" | | I want to protect the unborn. I want to protect your Second Amendment rights. I want to cut taxes,not raise them. I want to make sure America is NEVER a socialist country. But I can’t do any of those things in the House if I don’t make it – Sincerely, Jerry Evans | | | | | | | | | | | www.jerryforcongress.com | | | | | Our mailing address is: PO Box #572 Warrenville, IL 60555 Want to change how you receive these emails? name: Tina Smith
subject: Coronavirus
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
|
#
Friend — In the face of this growing public health crisis, I’m emailing you about the COVID-19 outbreak to share some helpful resources. This is important, so please read all the way through if you can. Here are a few important things you should do right now:
1. Wash your hands often with soap and warm water for at least 20 seconds (_that’s 20 seconds of my favorite, Lizzo, or two rounds of Happy Birthday_).
2. Don’t touch your face! I know it’s hard, but keep unwashed hands away from your eyes, nose, and mouth.
3. Always cover your cough or sneeze.
4. Disinfect frequently touched objects and surfaces. A few things that come to mind are your phone, laptop, remote controls, door knobs, light switches, and more.

For Minnesota-specific information on the virus, along with resources about keeping yourself and those around you safe, the **[Minnesota Department of Health](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16058527/170123666/1134587401?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTU1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhOWRlYzExMC03YzY1LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=AEYATB4BSl-9Qw3WgPv8sTbXi_2YJhfzkVpAci4BSdI=&emci=a8dec110-7c65-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=a9dec110-7c65-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=6227932)** website has everything you need. Otherwise, read more from the **[Centers for Disease Control](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16058528/170123667/-1368851855?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTU1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhOWRlYzExMC03YzY1LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=AEYATB4BSl-9Qw3WgPv8sTbXi_2YJhfzkVpAci4BSdI=&emci=a8dec110-7c65-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=a9dec110-7c65-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=6227932)** on how to stay healthy and safe. If you’re looking for ways to help communities across Minnesota during the outbreak, **[these are a few good resources to check out.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16058529/170123668/827696025?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTU1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhOWRlYzExMC03YzY1LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=AEYATB4BSl-9Qw3WgPv8sTbXi_2YJhfzkVpAci4BSdI=&emci=a8dec110-7c65-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=a9dec110-7c65-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=6227932)** Take care of yourselves. As our state and country work to make sure we have the resources and tests needed to slow the spread, we each must do our part to keep ourselves and our communities safe. We now have a common purpose, a common cause. Let's rise to it and stop the spread of this virus. Thanks for reading. And thank you to all of the health workers fighting this virus on the front lines and ensuring our communities get the care they need. — Tina
---

St. Paul, MN 55114

disclaimer_text: Paid for by Booker for Kentucky
body:
This is how Mitch is prioritizing his time instead of leading to protect Americans during a growing pandemic. He’s not helping to provide health care access to the most vulnerable or addressing the lack of access to COVID-19 testing — he’s going to a party.
#
The coronavirus outbreak has reached a critical turning point. When we need leadership the most, where does Mitch McConnell go?
****He gets on a flight with Brett Kavanaugh to go attend a reception for one of Kavanaugh’s fiercest defenders.****
This is how Mitch is prioritizing his time instead of leading to protect Americans during a growing pandemic. He’s not helping to provide health care access to the most vulnerable or addressing the lack of access to COVID-19 testing — he’s going to a party.
It’s never been more clear that we need someone who is the complete opposite of Mitch McConnell. Right now, Charles Booker is the only candidate who has made it clear that he’ll be the bold, people-first change we need.
[**Will you chip in $5 to stand with our campaign today? If we raise a record amount of money by our huge FEC deadline in 2 weeks it’ll send a huge message to the country & the party that people are ready to fight for change.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16058699/170124646/198153942?refcode=em20200313&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODg4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YzQ3NTdmNC03ZDY1LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=OAb5jBCRFCkMA-eeUYsXTkHyHIEAPKMAyTD8yWm42hI=)

We know these are difficult times.
It’s hard to act normal and go on with our day to day when there’s so much uncertainty about the future of our country.
But to be honest with you, that’s exactly what Mitch McConnell wants to happen. He hopes & wishes that we lose focus on our goals and that this progressive movement loses steam.
We refuse to let that happen. We believe it’s critical — especially in times like these — to stick together, stick up for our values, and fight for the change we need.
[**If you agree, will you chip you in anything you can today to our people-powered campaign so we can pull off a grassroots victory in 2 months? Team Booker

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: Five Stories President Trump Doesn’t Want You to Miss
body:
Trump's Coronavirus Travel Ban Makes Sense — Here's Why | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0bd328f811&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **_FIVE STORIES PRESIDENT TRUMP DOESN'T WANT YOU TO MISS_**
---
| | | [**Trump's Coronavirus Travel Ban Makes Sense — Here's Why**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=782caf226c&e=3ee4985eea)
-Fox News
“In dealing with pandemics, top priorities have to be limiting the spread of the disease and protecting our most vulnerable populations. Social distancing—that is, limiting the contact between people who are shedding the virus and others—is critical to achieving both goals. And that’s where travel bans have a role to play,” James Jay Carafano writes.

**MORE:** [“Trump is right: Suspend the payroll tax”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=929fc864a7&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| [**Trump Coronavirus Response Will Protect America’s Economy, Workers and Businesses**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=81b50cf294&e=3ee4985eea)
-Fox News
“President Trump addressed the nation Wednesday night and unveiled a two-pronged program of health and economic actions to deal with the coronavirus pandemic,” Andy Puzder writes. “The president’s action plan should win bipartisan support to respond to the most serious health and humanitarian crisis our nation has faced in my nearly seven decades.”

🇺🇸 **President Trump:**[“We are all in this together”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a5b125b5e2&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| [**150,000 Immigrants From 72 Nations With Coronavirus Stopped at Border**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5043cb4c1c&e=3ee4985eea)
_-Washington Examiner_
“Some 150,000 illegal immigrants from 72 nations with cases of the coronavirus have been apprehended or deemed inadmissible from entering the United States since November,” Paul Bedard reports. These apprehensions underscore the need for proper vetting and a safe legal immigration system as opposed to open-border policies.

**🏗****Progress:** [“New border wall blocks 90% of illegal crossings, up from just 10%”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=98724e0dbd&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
[**Pence: Major Health Insurers Have Agreed to Waive Copays for Coronavirus Tests**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f9b4944ef3&e=3ee4985eea)
-_The Hill_
“Vice President Pence said Tuesday that a group of major health insurance companies have agreed not to charge patients copays when they get tested for coronavirus,” Peter Sullivan reports. Alongside President Trump, “Pence made the comments at a meeting at the White House with the CEOs of major health insurance companies.”
---
|
---
|
[**Trump Administration Takes Action to Make Portable Electronic Health Records a Reality**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9f85a6060b&e=3ee4985eea)
-_USA Today_
“For many Americans, collecting and navigating their own health care records is a frustrating, time-consuming burden that leads to lower quality and higher costs,” write Jared Kushner, Seema Verma, and Dr. Don Rucker. “The Trump administration is now taking action to ensure that every American has secure access to their own health records.”
---

subject: I'm with Katie Porter>>
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Stand with Sánchez
body:
One thing is clear: our government needs to **step up** and protect the American public from the further spread of coronavirus (COVID-19).

That's why I'm grateful to have colleagues like Katie Porter beside me in the House, who are willing to **fight **to make the American people safer. Yesterday, Congresswoman Porter made headlines by getting a high-level Trump official to agree to make coronavirus testing **_free _**for all Americans. This is exactly the kind of leadership we need from our federal government right now.

[**Show Katie your support >>**](https://act.myngp.com/el/MO9ZAuNPO0N0fCUKVqQL3V-P9oC1JxRcQScBucn9wpw=/wzxPk5wPAZt-m-_ENFv0dB5YqY-x0QKvaP8rG1wz9vs=)

[](https://act.myngp.com/el/MO9ZAuNPO0N0fCUKVqQL3V-P9oC1JxRcQScBucn9wpw=/wzxPk5wPAZt-m-_ENFv0dB5YqY-x0QKvaP8rG1wz9vs=)

When she's not in Congress defending the American public, Katie is busy fighting to keep her seat Blue.

That won't be easy. Katie was the first Democrat to **EVER** win her district (CA-45). We need to make sure we keep her in Washington long after November 2020.

[**Let 's keep Katie right where she belongs -- in the House of Representatives>> **](https://act.myngp.com/el/MO9ZAuNPO0N0fCUKVqQL3V-P9oC1JxRcQScBucn9wpw=/wzxPk5wPAZt-m-_ENFv0dB5YqY-x0QKvaP8rG1wz9vs=)

Contributions made using this form will be split between Linda Sanchez for Congress and Katie Porter for Congress.
